items.headline: Auto Sales Dealership Proposed at 975 W. El Camino Real
items.summary: A special development permit was proposed to allow auto sales operations at an existing commercial property on El Camino Real. The project includes lot repaving, restriping, and landscaping but no building expansion or exterior modifications.

items.headline: 195-unit apartment complex permitted near Berryessa Transit Station
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development has received a building permit for 825 Berryessa Station Way, adding significant housing near public transit in the Berryessa area. The project represents one of San José's larger residential developments cleared for construction.

items.headline: City launches financial assistance pilot for water customers
items.summary: San José is introducing a Financial Assistance Pilot Program to help water customers manage utility costs. The program details and eligibility criteria were presented to council on May 12.

items.headline: Council to discuss litigation from Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a closed session on May 12 to confer with legal counsel about potential litigation arising from a water main incident at Cuesta Park. The city is also negotiating real property matters related to two parcels on Clyde Avenue.

items.headline: Planning Commission to review 27-unit townhome project with affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission is considering a residential development consisting of 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. The project will undergo design review and land-use evaluation at the May 12 meeting.

items.headline: Fantozzi litigation against city and SummerHill Homes ongoing
items.summary: The city is in active litigation with Mark Fantozzi (with SummerHill Homes, LLC as a real party in interest) in case 26CV492787. Details of the dispute were discussed in closed session.

items.headline: City Council to hear 72-unit affordable housing project at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: A public hearing is scheduled for a six-story, 100% affordable housing development with 72 units on a 0.43-acre site at 450 Lytton Avenue, which would replace a city-owned 52-space parking lot. The project is seeking density bonus concessions under state law.

items.headline: Council adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: Campbell City Council adopted a resolution prohibiting city-owned and city-operated property from being used for federal civil enforcement actions. This policy limits how federal agencies can access or utilize municipal facilities and resources.
